noveltyJustification: Distinguishes transient graph routing staleness from durable DB/file inconsistency and narrows entity-density staleness to update.

ruledOut.approach: Treat entity-density staleness as durable data corruption
ruledOut.reason: The cache feeds graph/degree channel preservation and has a 60s TTL; it does not rewrite stored memory rows.

ruledOut.approach: Treat all delete paths as missing entity-density invalidation
ruledOut.reason: The lower-level delete helper invalidates entity-density after successful deletes, and bulk-delete has explicit invalidation.

graphEvents.label: Do cache staleness and atomic-save ordering corrupt retrieval/routing under single-user operation?
memory_update can leave entity-density graph-routing cache stale until TTL or another invalidation.
Atomic save can commit index rows before final file promotion, creating durable DB/file inconsistency on crash or promotion failure.
